prerequisites: A running Apache Kafka cluster reachable from the connector, with one or more broker host:port pairs to set as bootstrap_servers.
One or more topics (or a topic pattern) to read, to configure under subscription.
A decision on the connection security protocol (PLAINTEXT, SASL_PLAINTEXT, SASL_SSL, or SSL) to set in protocol, since it is required.
If using SASL authentication, valid credentials (for example username and password, or a Kerberos principal) to embed in the SASL JAAS configuration string.
If consuming messages encoded against a Schema Registry, the Schema Registry URL and, when it is secured with HTTP basic auth, a username and password.

authMethods.name: SASL (sasl.jaas.config)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to brokers by setting the required protocol field to a SASL security protocol (SASL_PLAINTEXT or SASL_SSL) and providing the JAAS login configuration in the protocol.sasl_jaas_config secret. Apache Kafka supports the GSSAPI (Kerberos), PLAIN, SCRAM-SHA-256, SCRAM-SHA-512, and OAUTHBEARER mechanisms; the JAAS string carries the chosen mechanism's credentials. PLAIN and SCRAM should be used over SASL_SSL so passwords are not sent in clear text.

authMethods.name: Schema Registry basic auth (schema_registry_password)
authMethods.summary: When messages are decoded via a Schema Registry secured with HTTP basic authentication, supply the registry password in the MessageFormat.schema_registry_password secret alongside the registry URL and username. This is optional and only applies to the Schema Registry message format, not to broker authentication.

setupSteps.title: Set the bootstrap servers
setupSteps.body: In bootstrap_servers (required), enter a comma-separated list of one or more Kafka broker host:port pairs. The connector uses this list to discover the full cluster, so it does not need to contain every broker.

setupSteps.title: Choose the security protocol
setupSteps.body: Set protocol (required) to the cluster's connection security protocol. Apache Kafka supports PLAINTEXT, SSL, SASL_PLAINTEXT, and SASL_SSL. Use a SASL_ protocol when the cluster requires SASL authentication, and prefer SASL_SSL so credentials are encrypted in transit.

setupSteps.title: Provide SASL credentials
setupSteps.body: When protocol uses SASL, put the JAAS login configuration in the protocol.sasl_jaas_config secret. This string names the SASL mechanism (such as PLAIN, SCRAM-SHA-256, SCRAM-SHA-512, GSSAPI, or OAUTHBEARER) and its credentials, equivalent to Kafka's sasl.jaas.config client property.

setupSteps.title: Define the topic subscription
setupSteps.body: Configure subscription (required) to select which topics to consume, either by listing explicit topic names or by matching a topic pattern, depending on the subscription type the connector exposes.

setupSteps.title: Configure the message format (optional)
setupSteps.body: Use the optional MessageFormat field to set how records are decoded. If decoding against a Schema Registry that uses HTTP basic auth, provide the registry URL and username and place the password in MessageFormat.schema_registry_password.

setupSteps.title: Tune consumer behavior (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally adjust standard Kafka consumer settings exposed as config fields, including group_id, client_id, auto_offset_reset, enable_auto_commit, auto_commit_interval_ms, max_poll_records, request_timeout_ms, retry_backoff_ms, receive_buffer_bytes, client_dns_lookup, and polling_time, to control offset handling, batching, and timeouts.

sources.title: Use HTTP Basic Authentication in Confluent Platform (Schema Registry)
